explanation: The original command is modified by using the -Command parameter instead of the call operator (&), and by normalizing the path and escaping quotes. This reordering and substitution should still trigger the Sigma rule.
The command is wrapped in quotes and uses double backslashes for path normalization. The structure is preserved, and the Sigma rule should still detect it.
The command is rewritten using Get-ChildItem instead of ls (alias), and the -c parameter is used instead of -Command. This substitution and reordering should still match the Sigma rule.
The command is restructured by using the full cmdlet name and explicitly specifying the -Pattern parameter. This reordering and substitution should still match the Sigma rule.
The command uses an environment variable ($env:username) to dynamically reference the current user's directory. This substitution and recoding should still match the Sigma rule.
